text: Success is the product of daily habits—not once-in-a-lifetime transformations.
author: James Clear

text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
author: John Johnson

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: The best way to predict the future is to invent it.
author: Alan Kay

text: You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
author: James Clear

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: Software is a great combination between artistry and engineering.
author: Bill Gates

text: It's not that I'm so smart, it's just that I stay with problems longer.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Simplicity is the soul of efficiency.
author: Austin Freeman

text: Don't count the days, make the days count.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: Focus on being productive instead of busy.
author: Tim Ferriss

text: Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it’s bad.
author: Cory House

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Make it work, make it right, make it fast.
author: Kent Beck

text: The path to success is to take massive, determined action.
author: Tony Robbins

text: Be a yardstick of quality. Some people aren't used to an environment where excellence is expected.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Before software can be reusable it first has to be usable.
author: Ralph Johnson

text: Continuous improvement is better than delayed perfection.
author: Mark Twain

text: Your mind is for having ideas, not holding them.
author: David Allen

text: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
author: Linus Torvalds

text: Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment.
author: Jim Rohn

text: Quality is not an act, it is a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: One of my most productive days was throwing away 1000 lines of code.
author: Ken Thompson

text: The tragedy of life doesn't lie in not reaching your goal. The tragedy lies in having no goal to reach.
author: Benjamin E. Mays

text: Clean code always looks like it was written by someone who cares.
author: Michael Feathers

text: Energy and persistence conquer all things.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
author: Harold Abelson

text: Consistency is the true foundation of trust in yourself.
author: Unknown

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Action is the foundational key to all success.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Amateurs sit and wait for inspiration, the rest of us just get up and go to work.
author: Stephen King

text: Great development is 90% thinking and 10% writing code.
author: Unknown

text: If you cannot do great things, do small things in a great way.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: The best error message is the one that never shows up.
author: Thomas Fuchs

text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Small daily improvements over time lead to stunning results.
author: Robin Sharma

text: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation and naming things.
author: Phil Karlton

text: What gets measured gets managed.
author: Peter Drucker

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela
